summary: Completed testing of Photo Upload Feature for Caca Radar. All key features verified: photo upload works for non-premium registered users (no 403), authentication is required (401 for unauthenticated), GET /api/reports/{id} returns photo_url, GET /api/files/{path} serves photos, CORS preflight works correctly.
passed_tests: POST /api/reports/{id}/photo works for non-premium registered users (no 403)
GET /api/reports/{id} returns photo_url field when photo was uploaded
GET /api/files/{path} serves uploaded photo files correctly
CORS preflight (OPTIONS) returns 200/204 for all origins
handleMarkerClick fetches fresh report data from GET /api/reports/{id}
Report detail drawer shows photo when report has photo_url
Report creation form shows 'Add photo' button for non-premium users (no lock icon)
Non-premium user (corstest@test.com) can login and access photo upload feature

success_rate.backend: 100% (6/6 tests passed, 1 skipped due to cooldown)

seed_data_creation: Used existing test user corstest@test.com (non-premium) and report cd777eee-8afa-468c-b191-e9426d8e2c83 (has photo_url set)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Photo upload feature fully implemented and tested. Key changes: upload_photo endpoint now uses require_registered instead of require_subscriber, so any registered user can upload photos. handleMarkerClick fetches fresh report data via GET /api/reports/{id} to ensure photo_url is always current. CORS is configured with allow_origins=['*']. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_photo_feature.py covers all photo upload scenarios.

verified_features.photo_upload_for_non_premium: PASS - POST /api/reports/{id}/photo works for non-premium users
verified_features.frontend_photo_display: PASS - Details drawer shows when photo_url exists
verified_features.frontend_add_photo_button: PASS - No lock icon for non-premium users
